chief_complaint: A 60-year-old male presented with chronic cough and weight loss.
history: The patient has a history of severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), diabetes mellitus, and smoking. He presented with chronic cough and weight loss, prompting further investigation.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided.
labs: Imaging revealed a cavitary lesion in the left lung apex. Bronchoscopy and bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL) led to the identification of Purpureocillium lilacinum infection.
